name: Hoax.ai First Reference Candidate Pack
governing_principle: A candidate is not a page. It is a governed permission request for future reference value.

candidates.reference_thesis: Evidence posture is the governed condition of an evidence artifact before belief or action, not a truth verdict about subjects or events.
candidates.reference_purpose: Define evidence posture as a foundational reference concept linking taxonomy, standard, and protocol without implying detection or scoring capability.
candidates.definition_scope_summary: Covers artifact-level posture vocabulary, posture dimensions, and bounded use in governance language. Excludes live classifier, detector, upload, scoring, and fake-or-real verdict framing.
candidates.governance_boundary_summary: Must not be read as truth certification, subject accusation, public tool availability, or operational detector output.
candidates.claim_scope: Repository-supported governance and taxonomy claims only; no external factual statistics.
candidates.semantic_seo_role: Clarify governed meaning of evidence posture for a future reference page; no keyword stuffing or detector capture.
candidates.notes: Foundational candidate. Candidate only. No route or page created.

candidates.reference_thesis: Hoax.ai examines evidence artifacts and chains, not persons, institutions, or events as accusation targets.
candidates.reference_purpose: Document artifact–subject separation as a core governance boundary for all future reference pages and posture language.
candidates.definition_scope_summary: Defines separation between artifact posture and subject accusation. Excludes fraud determination, guilt language, and identity verdict framing.
candidates.governance_boundary_summary: Must not authorize subject accusation, institutional blame, or event verdict pages.
candidates.claim_scope: Governance claims from decision log and boundary documents only.
candidates.semantic_seo_role: Clarify governed boundary meaning; no accusation-oriented or detector keyword capture.

candidates.reference_thesis: Source confidence describes bounded trust posture toward source chains and provenance signals, not absolute truth or reputation scoring.
candidates.reference_purpose: Introduce source confidence as a governed reference framework concept within evidence posture vocabulary.
candidates.definition_scope_summary: Covers source chain examination, provenance signal context, and uncertainty markers. Excludes numeric reputation scores and external factual claims about real entities.
candidates.governance_boundary_summary: Must not imply scoring engine, truth certification, or external authority claims about real organizations.
candidates.claim_scope: Internal taxonomy and governance claims only.
candidates.semantic_seo_role: Clarify governed source confidence meaning for future reference comprehension.
candidates.notes: Framework candidate. Batch 1 production page created.

candidates.reference_thesis: A provenance gap is a governed signal that custody, origin, or chain-of-custody cannot be meaningfully examined, not an accusation of forgery or fraud.
candidates.reference_purpose: Define provenance gap as a reference framework term within posture and taxonomy language.
candidates.definition_scope_summary: Covers gap signals in provenance dimensions. Excludes forgery verdicts, fraud accusations, and detector claims.
candidates.governance_boundary_summary: Must not be read as forgery determination or subject accusation.
candidates.claim_scope: Taxonomy and standard governance claims only.
candidates.semantic_seo_role: Clarify governed provenance gap meaning without detector or fake-real keyword capture.
candidates.notes: Framework candidate. Batch 1 production page created.

candidates.reference_thesis: Not assessable is a bounded posture state indicating insufficient evidence structure for confident posture assignment, not a hidden verdict of falsehood.
candidates.reference_purpose: Document the not assessable posture state as a governed taxonomy reference unit.
candidates.definition_scope_summary: Covers taxonomy state definition, sufficiency boundaries, and uncertainty communication. Excludes binary fake-real framing.
candidates.governance_boundary_summary: Must not imply classifier output, scoring, or truth machine behavior.
candidates.claim_scope: Taxonomy and standard mapped governance claims only.
candidates.semantic_seo_role: Clarify governed posture state meaning; no fake-real or detector SEO framing.
candidates.notes: Posture state candidate. Batch 1 production page created at /reference/not-assessable/.

candidates.reference_thesis: The output boundary schema defines the language contract for any future engine output without creating a public tool or classifier.
candidates.reference_purpose: Document output boundary as a governed reference unit connecting schema, protocol, and engine model boundaries.
candidates.definition_scope_summary: Covers schema fields, prohibited output classes, and language contracts. Excludes API documentation, product pages, and classifier marketing.
candidates.governance_boundary_summary: Must not imply software product, API availability, or live classifier output.
candidates.claim_scope: Schema and governance file claims only.
candidates.semantic_seo_role: Clarify governed output boundary meaning for future technical reference comprehension.
candidates.notes: Output boundary candidate. Batch 1 production page created.

candidates.reference_thesis: Claim and source traceability requires every material reference claim to map to governed sources before public release.
candidates.reference_purpose: Document claim and source traceability as a reference governance unit for future pages.
candidates.definition_scope_summary: Covers claim policy, evidence ledger, claim-source map, and source registry discipline. Excludes external factual claims without mapping.
candidates.governance_boundary_summary: Must not authorize unmapped external claims or invented citations on reference pages.
candidates.claim_scope: Governance and traceability policy claims only.
candidates.semantic_seo_role: Clarify traceability governance meaning; no authority manufacturing through metadata.

candidates.reference_thesis: Synthetic fragility names the collapse of easy confidence in the evidence layer when plausibility can be manufactured at scale.
candidates.reference_purpose: Document synthetic fragility as a category thesis reference unit aligned with homepage governed language.
candidates.definition_scope_summary: Covers category thesis vocabulary and evidence-layer uncertainty. Excludes current events, real entity examples, and detector product framing.
candidates.governance_boundary_summary: Must not become a news page, accusation page, or tool landing page.
candidates.claim_scope: Internal category and governance claims only.
candidates.semantic_seo_role: Clarify governed category thesis meaning; no keyword stuffing or detector capture.
candidates.notes: Category thesis framework candidate. Candidate only.
